repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, diaries, financial records, manuscripts, printed material, publications, maps, architectural plans, and artwork.
did.abstract: Papers include student drawings, business and personal correspondence, subject files, account books, diaries, postcards, clippings, notebooks, lecture slides, course material and exams, notes and drafts of articles, reports, printed material concerning Sardis, pamphlets, maps, charts, blueprints, floor plans, and other material pertaining to Detweiler's work at Cornell, the American Schools of Oriental Research, history of architecture, and archaeological field work.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Albert Henry Detweiler received a bachelor's degree in architecture in 1930 from the University of Pennsylvania. In the early 1930s he became involved with the American Schools of Oriental Research, with which he maintained a lifelong affiliation. He joined the Cornell University faculty in 1939, was appointed associate dean of the College of Architecture in 1956, and as chairman of the Committee on Student Conduct was active in the organization of a new judicial system at Cornell. In 1957 Professor Detweiler joined George M. A. Hanfmann of Harvard in organizing the archaeological exploration of Sardis in Turkey under the aegis of the American Schools of Oriental Research. Detweiler was also field archaeologist at a number of famous Near Eastern excavations, was interested in architectural conservation and restoration, and studied architectural history in Europe, including study of the origins of Christian architecture while a Guggenheim Fellow in 1961-1962. He was active in professional organizations, including the Society of Architectural Historians and the American Institute of Architects. He was architect and archaeologist at Dura, Samaria, and Tell Beit Mirsim; architectural advisor at Seleucia; surveyed the Great Mosque in Isfahan, Iran; and architect at Bostra in 1935.
Letter from Henry Detweiler to Deane W. Malott about Detweiler's participation in the 600th anniversary of the founding of the University of Pavia, 1961.

emph.ref: Series I. American School of Oriental Research (ASOR) Material
Series II. Architecture and Course Materials
Series IV. General Documents and Material
Series V. American Research Institute in Turkey (ARIT) Materials
Series VI. American Research Center in Egypt (ARCE) Materials
Series VII. American Institute for Architects (AIA) Materials

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: A. Henry Detweiler. Papers, #15-2-1363. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers include student drawings, business and personal correspondence, subject files, account books, diaries, postcards, clippings, notebooks, lecture slides, course material and exams, notes and drafts of articles, reports, printed material concerning Sardis, pamphlets, maps, charts, blueprints, floor plans, and other material pertaining to Detweiler's work at Cornell, the American Schools of Oriental Research, history of architecture, and archaeological field work. Also, approximately 120 condolence letters written to Catharine Bunnell Detweiler after the death of her husband.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Letter from Henry Detweiler to Deane W. Malott about Detweiler's participation in the 600th anniversary of the founding of the University of Pavia, 1961.

Architects.
Architecture.
Archaeological expeditions.

did.unittitle: Series I. American School of Oriental Research (ASOR) Material
ASOR Correspondence on New Jerusalem Building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes expense reports and publication proposals for excavation of the tomb of Antiochus at Nimrud Dagh.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes about Madaba, correspondence, financial reports, reports of the physical condition of the school, and certificate appointing Detweiler as Acting Director.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and notebook with notes and drawings of palace near Jericho.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of this correspondence is with Carl Kraeling, J.L. Kelso, and Helene Kantor. Includes photographs of the Jericho excavation (1950) from Kelso and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding funding and drafts of reports and articles on Jericho.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Board of Trustee correspondence and copies of meeting minutes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes undated stereoview photograph of Cornell University's Arts Quad, showing Morrill and McGraw Halls.

did.unittitle: Series II. Architecture and Course Materials
Cornell College of Architecture New York City Program
American Institute of Architects of Gatlinburg Conference
National Trust for Historic Preservation Materials
Spain, Netherlands, Germany, and Scandinavia Renaissance Architecture Notes
Dutch, German, English Colonial Architecture Notes
Italian and Spanish Gothic Architecture Notes
English and German Gothic Architecture Notes
Classic Revival in the US Notes
Spanish and French Colonies in America Notes
Romanesque in Germany, Scandinavia, and Spain Notes
History of Engineering in Architecture Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of archaeological excavation and unidentified interiors and exteriors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Detweiler's architectural drawings and related papers from course reports.

did.unittitle: Siena and Orvieto Charts of Historic Events
Notes on Business and Architecture Styles
Scroll Presented to AHD in Puerto Rico
History of American and Contemporary Art Syllabus Notes
History of Architecture 413 American Architecture Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of old Ithaca houses and Carl Tallman photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negatives of Ithaca chapel at Green street and Cayuga street, a copy of Cornell Reading-Courses, Ross House at Phelphs New York, report on the Murray House at Union Springs, stereoviews of Cornell University buildings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Largely consists of building exteriors, with some maps.

did.unittitle: Cornell Committee on Special Education Projects (COSEP)
George V Egge Jr and Fowler Hamilton Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes personal correspondence and newspaper clippings.

did.unittitle: Business correspondence, applications for jobs, and biography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes three unidentified photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negative photographs of aerial view of Seleucia,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various pamphlets and brochures, handwritten report for Cornell, annotated map of the UK, and 1792 "Bref Examen, Des Qualite's de L'acier Fondu de Huntsman."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the material relates to the publication of Detweiler's book. Includes correspondence with W.F. Albright.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and Site Plans- Ann Perkins

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Site Plan of Roman building at Dura?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, site plans, and reports for Dura excavation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Kathleen Kenyon.

International Council of Monuments and Sites (ICOMOS) Correspondence
Correspondence- A.H. Detweiler to C.S. Detweiler

did.unittitle: Correspondence- A.H. Detweiler to C.S. Detweiler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from W.F. Albright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sketches, photographs, and drafts for the manual.

Correspondence- A.H. Detweiler to C.S. Detweiler

Series IV. General Documents and Material
Campus Store Board of Directors Notes
Preservation of Environmental Values on the Campus
Vice President for Student Affairs Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes map of Amman, Jordanian Antiquities map by the Ministry of Public Buildings and Works (1963), and map showing Detweiler's travels in the 1930s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes student grade book, photographs of Minas Gerais in Brazil, correspondence, and notes.

did.unittitle: Architecture of Gramercy Park, student paper by Bruce Green
Class notes and general notes, mostly on the history of architecture
Class notes and general notes, mostly on the history of architecture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1950-1951 expense book, which includes loose photograph of Samara, and an English translation of Italian transistor radio instructions written by Detweiler (1962).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely school notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely school notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notebook of original photographs from Jerusalem, Homs, Palmyra, Dura, and Ain Ali. It also contains sketches and notes.

did.unittitle: Medal from Pavia, Italy (?) in blue bag
Progress Report by Watertown, NY City Planning Commission
Encyclopedie ou Dictionnaire Raisonne des Sciences, des Arts, et des Metiers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Arabic map of Jordan and occupied territory of Palestine, map of Phoenicia, and Transjordan map.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Memorial of the International Exhibition at Philadelphia (1876), Detweiler's ration book (1947), University of Pennsylvania student material (1925-1926, signed photograph of William F. Albright, and Detweiler's last architectural design (1969).

did.unittitle: An Interim Report on the City of Bethlehem
Award given to The University of Pavia
Lecture given to Saturday Night Club
Architecture Lecture II and III and V Slides: The Levant
Architecture Lecture I Slides: Maps, Dura, and Sardis Excavations
Architecture Lecture I and IV Slides: Jerash (Gerasa)
Paintings and drawings of building and ruins
Paintings and drawings of building and ruins
Class of 1930 University of Pennsylvania architectural school photograph
Series V. American Research Institute in Turkey (ARIT) Materials
Series VI. American Research Center in Egypt (ARCE) Materials
Pharaonic Monuments in the U.A.R. Report
Series VII. American Institute for Architects (AIA) Materials
Series VIII. Personal Material and Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ration books, Detweiler's highschool report cards and art project, University of Pennsylvania report cards, and selective service cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of family (1897-1912), North Bridgeport (1903), California and the west coast (1903), Niagara Falls (1900), The Catskills (1904), Bermuda (1905), California (1910), Connecticut (1897, 1911), Virginia, Florida (1913), North Carolina and Georgia (1914), Nassau (1913)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Antwerp, Holland, and France (1905-1908); Avignon (1911-1918); Algiers, Constantine, Timgad, the Sahara (1911); Arizona, California, Colorado (1910, 1915); Pan Pacific International Exposition (1915); California (1916-1923).

did.unittitle: Papers associated with Detweiler's attempt to get Naval Commission
